One-Time Cleansing of Property, Plant, and Equipment in accordance with

4.10 We recommended and Management agreed that:

Report of Unserviceable Property with the accounting records and
subsequently submits it to the Disposal Committee;
4.10.2 The Disposal Committee conducts an ocular inspection based on the

submit the IIRUP, Inspection Report, and Appraisal Report to the
Audit Team for review; and
4.10.3 Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA-DBM Joint Circular No.
2024-01 to ensure the proper and timely disposal of
unserviceable properties.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to require the concerned contractors
to provide warranty securities for structural defects as required in Section
62.2.3.3, effective one year from the end of the defects liability period, to
ensure sufficient financial coverage for potential structural defects during
the warranty period.

6.9 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant in
coordination with the Municipal Engineer immediately submit the As-built
plan of the completed infrastructure projects and ensure the complete
documentation of future final payments for infrastructure projects.

7.9 We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRMO Designate
prepare and submit the Report of Fuel Consumption to the Audit Team and
provide the driver’s trip tickets to the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure
proper accountability for fuel utilization, in compliance with the
requirements of COA Circular No. 77-61.

8.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
require the submission of lacking documents and henceforth strengthen its
management controls over the procurement of drugs and medicines by
ensuring that all disbursement vouchers are supported with complete
documents to establish the propriety, regularity, and validity of the claims.

9.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Health Officer
ensure that only drugs and medicines listed on PNF Volume I or the Essential
Medicines List are requested. However, if it is necessary to procure drugs
and medicines that are not on the list, then send a request with justification
to the National Drug Policy Office.

10.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Health Officer
ensure that the purchase requests do not specify brand names but instead
state generic names and that the Bids and Awards Committee invite
personnel from the Rural Health Unit with technical knowledge of medicines
during BAC Meetings on procuring drugs and medicines.

11.10 We recommended and Management agreed to assign a duly registered and
licensed pharmacist to the ALERT Loreto 24/7 unit or ensure that all
prescription medicines are dispensed solely at the Rural Health Unit, where
a pharmacist is stationed.

12.11.1 The Municipal Accountant reconcile the subsidiary ledgers under the
Due to GSIS account, particularly those with negative balances, and
effect the necessary adjustments in the books. Additionally, the
₱4,002,783.58 variance should be thoroughly reconciled and any
discrepancies between the amounts withheld by the LGU and the
amounts billed by GSIS should be immediately communicated to the
Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to facilitate timely
reconciliation with GSIS; and
12.11.2 The HRMO, through the Agency Authorized Officer, coordinate with
the Government Service Insurance System to reconcile premium
contributions and loan amortizations for the concerned officials and
employees, ensuring accurate recording and remittance of
obligations.

Treasurer prioritize the monthly reconciliation of their records.
Additionally, the Municipal Accountant should use the prescribed Bank
Reconciliation Statement form, that requires reconciling the balances per

the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the accounts with negative
balances against the available accounting and treasury records to
correct the erroneous entries in the subsidiary ledgers. Furthermore,
apply all possible means in tracing and retrieving the source
documents to establish the beginning balances of those accounts that

necessary documents in relation to the donated motor vehicles and

Install measures to strengthen the treasury’s internal control to avoid recurrence of delayed remittances of collections thereby preventing occurrence of shortages during cash examination thus, allowing the Municipality the immediate use of its financial resources to fund its priority development programs, projects and activities; and
The Municipal Treasurer commented that measures will be established to prevent the same violations from recurring.

We recommended that:

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical
inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year, and prepare
and submit Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) to the
Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;
b. Direct the GSO to prepare Request and Issue Slip in every issuance of
inventory item and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and
Materials Issued to MAO, maintain a stock cards for inventories and
conduct periodic reconciliation of their records with the ledger cards
of the MAO; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to use the perpetual inventory
system in the recording of inventories.

2.6. We recommended that the Management:
a. Require the Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination
with the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of
all Local Roads and a Road Map and prepare RLRN and RPCLRN
yearly; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adjusting journal entries
to recognize changes upon completion of the required inventory
reports and provide depreciation expense accordingly.

3.11. We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that Management:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count and do
it earlier to give enough time to prepare the RPCPPE, so any
discrepancy found in the accounting and property records may be
reconciled as soon as possible;
b. Require the Accountant and the GSO – Designate to exert efforts to
compare their respective records and verify/trace the unreconciled
balances and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any
unrecorded/ erroneous recording of PPEs to come up with the correct
balances of the accounts; and
c. Augment personnel to GSO to enable to execute their mandated duties
and functions.

4.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to adjust the Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs accounts to
reflect the correct balance as of December 31, 2020. Henceforth, ensure
proper recording of transactions for the fair presentation of the accounts
in the financial statements.

5.6. We recommended that the Management immediately designate another
personnel who is not involved in the collection function/activities to act as
Special Disbursing Officer to avoid mishandling of government funds, thus
providing the Municipality of a sound internal control pursuant to the
provisions of Section 124 of P.D. 1445 and Section 50 of GAAM Volume
III.

6.5. We recommended that the Management instruct the MPDC and the
Municipal Engineer to coordinate, prepare and submit duly accomplished
list of all on-going government infrastructure and non-infrastructure
PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance
to COA Circular No. 2013-004 for the timely and thorough verification of
the PPAs’ status.

7.13. We recommended that the Management submit the necessary lacking
documents and adhere to Sections 34.8, 37.1 and 37.4.1 of the Revised IRR
of RA 9184.

8.8. We recommended that the Management strictly follow the reporting and
posting requirements related to government procurement and observe
strict compliance thereof to ensure transparency and accountability.

9.7. We recommended that the Management direct the BAC to thoroughly
check the eligibility requirements of prospective contractors and/or
suppliers and observe adherence to the provisions of the abovementioned
circular and IRR.

10.8. We recommended that the Management adhere to the guidelines in
posting and submit a copy and written notices of the monthly utilization
report and status of implementation of PPAs under the BGCM fund and
the oversight agencies in compliance with Item 4.4 of DBM LBC No. 125.

11.7. We recommended that Management prepare and issue a well-defined set
of guidelines/policy governing grant of any financial assistance sourced out
of the LGU fund, require all applicants to submit the necessary documents
to establish identification of the claimants and the validity and legality of
the claims, and faithfully adhere to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445
in the implementation of any program of the LGU.

12.6. We recommended that Management employs means to ensure that all
contracts are submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working
days from the perfection thereof and require the personnel in-charge to
submit the delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance pursuant
to the above-mentioned regulations.

13.6. We recommended that management stop the practice of charging items
that are not related to disaster risk reduction and management and adhere
strictly to the provisions of Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121 in the
utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
(LDRRMF) of the Municipality.

14.7. We recommended that Management: (1) Post the reports on the receipt
and utilization of DRRMF sourced from GAA and donations in cash and
in-kind in at least three conspicuous places of the Municipality; (2)
Instruct the GSO to coordinate with the MDRRMO and other offices with
regard the donated items for the maintenance of an inventory list thereof;
(3) Direct the MDRRMO to prepare the required summary list together
with the required supporting documents and submit the same promptly to
COA; (4) Require the MDRRMO/Municipal Accountant to prepare and
submit to COA the reports on cash donations and in-kind donations; and
(5) Adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular No.
2020-009 on the accounting and reporting guidelines of cash and in-kind
donations.

1.7 	We recommended that the Management maintain Stock Cards and Property Cards to account the inventories and PPEs of the Municipality respectively. Likewise, we recommended creating an inventory committee to ensure the conduct of physical count of both assets. The inventory committee to undertake the physical count, prepare the report, reconcile the report with the records of the accounting office and submit to the Office of the Auditor to ensure that the reported amounts in the Financial Statements are fairly presented.
1.8 	We also recommended to set-up a General Services Office if warranted.

2.7	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record purchases of supplies as inventory and subsequently record it as expense upon distribution or consumption. Moreover, prepare necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the two accounts in the financial statements.

3.8 	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer retrieve all stale checks and the corresponding DVs together with all supporting documents, and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the said documents for the preparation of the journal entry voucher.  Replacement checks shall be issued following the procedures in accordance with the above provision of the GAM, Vol. I.

4.8 	We recommended that the BAC check the eligibility requirements of the prospective bidders and have an active list of the roaster of bidders of the Municipality and post the required procurement information in the website of the Municipality and/or in at conspicuous places in the premises of the procuring entity and at GPPB online portal in adherence to the provisions of the above - mentioned Circular.
4.9 	Also, the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the supporting documents before the submission of DVs for approval for payment by the Municipal Mayor.

5.6 	We recommended and management agreed to designate a Property Officer who shall prepare Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, supported with Acknowledgement Receipt, Issuance Form and inventory of remaining undistributed items for prospective COVID-19 related in-kind donations received within ten (10) working days after the end of each quarter in compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009.
5.7	Refrain from delegating to the end-user the responsibility to receive and dispose the subject donations in order to achieve sound internal control and proper accounting thereof.

6.5 	We recommended that the Local Finance Committee to diligently prepare the monthly utilization report for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities and post the same to the LGU’s website and in at least three conspicuous places in the locality. Also send written notices, that said reports were posted, to the respective government agencies where the submission is required in compliance with Items 4.2 to 4.4 of DBM LBC 125 to properly monitor and establish transparency in the utilization of the fund.

7.6 	We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to demand immediate liquidation from accountable officers concern and refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers with unliquidated balance.    For accounts that fall within the period of dormancy, we also recommend that management request an authority to write-off accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

8.5	We recommended to direct the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in prescribed format in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 on or before the 15th day after the end of the month.

9.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish a Special Trust Fund Account “Trust Liability-DRRMF” for the transfers of Agency’s unutilized QRF and DRRM-MOOE, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF from other LGUs and other sources. Likewise, maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for the transfers/receipts of fund.

10.5 	We recommended that Management ensure, the use of government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a driver’s trip ticket, which should be serially numbered and should bear the approval of the Head of Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence
10.6	Moreover, ensure that all payments of fuel expenses are completely documented with the duly accomplished and approved driver’s trip tickets and fuel consumption reports.   Data on speedometer readings, names of passengers, and vehicle used, the plate number among others shall also be indicated, so that fuel consumption can be regulated and the reasonableness of the usage can be properly determined.

11.7	We recommended to refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures to Special Education Fund and strictly adhere with the regulations provided in Section 272, Chapter 7 of Republic Act No. 7160 and DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated January 19, 2017 in the utilization of SEF.

12.6 	We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System and the Municipal Planning and Development Council initiate the establishment of GAD database and update the same in adherence to the provisions of RA 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

13.5	We recommended that the Budget Officer ensures the timely preparation and submission of the required Financial Accountability Reports on the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations, Disbursements and Balances in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-3 of COA and DBM, in order for the audit team to conduct timely review and evaluation of the Municipality’s Financial Accountability.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or reconciling items or errors detected are adjusted and posted in their respective books.
b. Record and adjust reconciling items immediately in its respective books all reconciling items found to be valid.
c. Follow-up the request/demand letter from the concerned depository banks copies of the CMs and DMs to determine their validity and effect the necessary adjustments.
d. Review and analyze the prior years’ reconciling items and locate the supporting documents especially of the bank debits and unrecorded vouchers to validate the issuance thereof.
e. Closely supervise the assigned personnel especially those assigned in the recording of the books of the treasurer and accounting, as well as those who will prepare the BRS. Thoroughly review and scrutinize documents for the proper booking up of the reconciling items.

observations.recommendation:
1.8	We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to:
Conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.
Record and adjust immediately in its respective books all reconciling items found to be valid.
Follow-up the request/demand letter from the concerned depository banks copies of the CMs and DMs to determine their validity and effect the necessary adjustments.
Review and analyze the prior years’ reconciling items and locate the supporting documents especially of the bank debits and unrecorded vouchers to validate the issuance thereof.
Closely supervise the assigned personnel especially those assigned in the recording of the books of the treasurer and accounting, as well as those who will prepare the BRS. Thoroughly review and scrutinize documents for the proper booking up of the reconciling items.

2.4	We recommended that the Management direct those concerned officials to conduct inventory taking of the subject inventories and submit the report to the Accounting Office for reconciliation. Any discrepancy shall be investigated and traced to the source documents and effect the necessary adjustments to the respective records in order to reflect the correct balance of said inventories account in the financial statements.

3.7 We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment by providing the quantity per actual count on the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit the same to the Audit Team.

4.8	We recommended that the Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and to hasten the implementation of these projects/activities funded from National Government Agencies especially those releases in earlier years, so that the constituents can benefit from said projects. Likewise, require those concerned officials who are in-charge in the inspection of the completed projects to submit the necessary documents to the accounting office in order to reflect/adjust the correct unutilized balance of the Due to NGAs account. Moreover, kindly submit justification on the non-utilization of the remaining funds and return to source agency any unused balance of grants/fund transfer upon completion of the project.

5.10	We recommended that the procuring entity furnish a copy of the contract within five (5) days upon perfection to the COA Office as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
5.11	Likewise, please submit to COA the lacking documents as enumerated above, to enable us to review the contract in its entirety. All documents shall be properly authenticated or clear certified as true copies from the original otherwise, the same shall be returned to that end without action.
5.12	Further, please be informed that auditorial contract review is required in the technical review and inspection by the Technical Information and Technology Services Office, COA Regional Office No. XIII, Butuan City.

6.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct those concerned officials to submit the disbursement vouchers together with the complete supporting documents within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

7.8	We recommended that the Management exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds. Moreover, the practice of letting other associations organize the activities of the Municipality to skip the procurement process of the government should be discouraged.

8.6	We recommended that the Management adhere to COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the installation of Billboards/Signages for the information and publicity of Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the Municipality to promote transparency and involvement of the public in the transaction entered by the government.
8.7	We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant will require as part of the supporting documents the pictures of the signage being placed in the construction site for claims of mobilization fee as the case may be.

9.7	We recommended that the Management instruct the MENRO to develop more its implementation of Solid Waste Management in accordance with the provisions stated in RA 9003 otherwise known as Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

9.8	It is also recommended that the MENRO implement and search for other waste diversion methods that could help minimize or mitigate the possible hazardous effects of the non-establishment of Sanitary Landfill.

11.11	We recommended that the Management observe prudence in the incurrence of expenditures considering the scarce resources of the government and require those concerned officials with issued mobile phones to refund the cost of the said purchase, considering that they are already enjoying cellphone allowances at Php2,500.00 per month.

12.7	We recommended that the Management exercise prudence in evaluating expenditures that are eligible for funding out of the Confidential Fund pursuant to Item 3.23 of Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of the Commission on Audit (COA), Department of Budget and Management (DBM), Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG), Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of National Defense (DND). Further, we recommended that the concerned officials review the correctness of the amount of the CF and render satisfactory explanation of the said condition.

13.5	We recommended that the Management direct those involved in planning and implementation to monitor closely and hasten the implementation of the projects. Likewise, optimally utilize the different funds allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development so that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

15.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
direct those concerned officials to meticulously prepare and review the POW to ensure that only the required number of materials are included in the plan to avoid unnecessary costs; and
require those concerned officials in the preparation, review and approval of the POW to refund the deficiencies noted in the amount of Php60,759.34; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the refund.

1.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the accounts affected in order to present fairly the Due From Other Funds/Due to Other Funds Accounts in the year-end FS.
2.5 	We recommended that Job Order Employees submit daily accomplishment reports in accordance with the above-cited provisions.

3.6 	We recommended that the Management exert utmost effort to require the concerned officials and employees to turn-over and/or return the properties received by them to the property custodian and for the latter to submit to the Audit Team the turn-over documents for verification.

4.5 	We recommended that the Management require the Local Chief Executive to issue an executive order to reconstitute and strengthen the GFPS in order to be fully able to lead GAD mainstreaming efforts with enhanced capabilities on gender sensitivity and gender responsive planning in strict compliance with the afore-cited JMC 2013-01.

5.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compel the concerned Punong Barangays of its failure to submit the required documents and to inform in writing after 5 days the Auditor if the same are not complied despite receipt of the Municipal Accountant’s letter.

6.7	We recommended that Management compel the Barangay Officials concerned to effect immediately the liquidation of the amount transferred thereto by not making subsequent fund transfers to their favor unless previous fund transfers were fully liquidated.

7.4	We recommended that Management establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA in order to provide management with the much-needed data & information essential for policy making.

8.6	We recommended that Management direct the   Sanggunian Bayan to revisit and review the Municipal Revenue Code and ensure that the taxes imposed are not in violation to the prescribed business tax rates prescribed in the LGC.

9.5	We recommended that Management utilized the Multi-purpose Shredder and Glass Pulverizer equipment to achieve the purpose of procuring the said equipment, as embodied in the Solid Waste Management Plan, and in order to fully maximize its usage and derive the expected   benefits therefrom.

10.7	We recommended that Management avail of a computerized Real Property Tax System that would be able to  handle  the needed  data of properties and taxpayers in order that  a more reliable, complete, and certified list of real property tax collectibles and other reports would be generated on time.
10.8 	Further, we recommended that henceforth, the LGU consistently post the notice of delinquency in a conspicuous place within the premises of the municipal hall in compliance with the foregoing provision of the LGC.

11.6	We recommended that the concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate and render account for their long overdue Cash Advances or refund in favor of the government the unliquidated amount, if applicable.
11.7	Further, we recommended that Management refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers whose previous cash advances were not yet liquidated.

We recommended that Management:
Issue demand letters to all accountable officers to settle all unliquidated cash advances. In case of failure, cause the withholding or suspension of their salaries;
Request write-off of receivables for those AOs who were already dead and can no longer be traced after exerting extra effort in tracing said AOs pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Institute appropriate controls for employees to settle their accountabilities before their separation from the service from the Municipality.
Strictly adhere to Section 89 of PD 1445, Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Item 6.0 and 9.0 of COA Circular 2012-004.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct those
concerned officials to submit the disbursement vouchers together with the
complete supporting documents within the prescribed period pursuant to
Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular 2009-006.

2.13 We recommended that Management (1) exhaust all options
available to secure immediately the liquidation report of long outstanding
downloaded funds to determine the purpose for which the funds were
transferred or carried out and demand the return of fund if found out not
fully utilized; (2) after trying out all the alternatives to obtain the liquidation
report but was unable to do so, request the write-off of dormant accounts to
clear the books of the Agency; and (3) it is also recommended to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to its proper account on the
financial assistance given to Liga ng mga Barangay amounting to P7,500.00.

3.10 We recommended that Management:
a. Issue demand letters to all accountable officers to settle all
unliquidated cash advances. In case of failure, cause the
withholding or suspension of their salaries;
b. Request write-off of receivables for those AOs who were already
dead and can no longer be traced after exerting extra effort in
tracing said AOs pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.
c. Institute appropriate controls for employees to settle their
accountabilities before their separation from the service from the
Municipality.
d. Strictly adhere to Section 89 of PD 1445, Section 5 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 and Item 6.0 and 9.0 of COA Circular 2012-
004.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipality undertake the following
measures:
1) perform periodic reconciliation of stock/property cards to
ensure that discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared
and reconciled with the books of accounts.
2) instruct the Inventory Committee to exert more effort to
complete the conduct of physical inventory of all PPE accounts
and classify each item found during inventory taking to its
appropriate account;
3) prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly
investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct
account balances; and
4) instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to continue its
effort to locate pertinent source documents to reduce the
unidentified balances in the property ledger cards, if any, in
order to render a complete and accurate financial report.

5.6 We recommended that Management direct those involved in
planning and implementation to monitor closely and to hasten the
implementation of these projects/activities funded from National
Government Agencies especially those releases in earlier years, so that the
constituents can benefit from said projects. Likewise, please submit
justification on the non-utilization of the funds. Return to source agency any
unused balance of grants/fund transfer upon completion of the project.

6.5 We recommended that Management:
1) exert effort to follow up the liquidation reports particularly
those long outstanding amounts and send demand letters to
NGAs concerned and required the immediate refund of the
unspent amount;
2) conduct investigation on the causes of delays in the submission
of the liquidation reports by concerned NGAs which attributed
to the delay in the completion and/or non-implementation of
the projects, programs and activities as intended and
recommend possible action to address such undue delays;
3) Adhere to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994

7.5 We recommended that the Management:
1. instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the
corresponding liquidated damages amounting to Php13,387.38
from the amount due to the concerned contractor or to demand
payment for such amount, as the case may be as a result of the
apparent delay of the completion of the project; and
2. direct those concerned officials in the implementation of the
projects to give emphasis on the monitoring and supervision of
all on-going and upcoming projects specifically as to the
completion of the projects on time or as plan and render
satisfactory explanation on the deficiencies noted in the COA
technical inspection of the project.

1.7	We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant - Designate to submit immediately the required BRSs. Strictly comply with the requirement on the submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements as provided in Item 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 and submit the same, together with the bank statements, on or before due dates to the Office of the Auditor. Furthermore, periodic reconciliation of cash balances shall likewise be conducted by the municipal treasurer and the municipal accountant.
2.7 	We recommended that the municipal accountant reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds Accounts to present fairly the financial position of the municipality.
We recommended that Management instruct the Treasurer or his authorized representative to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents after the release of checks.
4.6 	Direct Management to strictly follow the provisions of the IRR of RA 10752 in the future procurement of real properties as prescribed under section 4.4 (b) of the IRR of RA 9184.

5.6 	We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports/documents enumerated in Section 4.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.
6.6 	Direct the MPDO to submit the AIP regularly to the DILG Regional Office and DBM in compliance with Section 6 of the afore-cited JMC.

Direct the Management to prioritize inclusion in the Project Procurement Management Plan of the ensuing year the personal protective equipment and supplies of the garbage collectors in compliance with Section 23(a) of RA 9003.

8.6 	We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Systems appropriately identify PAPs that are gender-responsive and gender-sensitive for inclusion to its GAD Plan and stop disbursing GAD Funds to PAPs that are not related to gender and development.
8.7 	We also recommended that payment for PhilHealth premiums of the indigents be charged to the appropriate fund of the Municipality.
9.6 	Direct Management to include in the LDRRMP of the ensuing year activities and projects under disaster rehabilitation and post recovery which are not yet included in the previous plan and budget.

10.6 	We recommended that Management instruct those in charge of the payroll preparation and those in charge for the contribution of mandatory deductions to properly compute the Pag-IBIG premiums at Php100.00 for employer’s share per employee.
10.7 	Further, strictly follow the maximum ceiling of Five Thousand Pesos (Php5,000.00) monthly to be used in the computation of employer share as provided under Section 7 of RA 9679, unless amended.
10.8 	Furthermore, for the meantime, stop paying for the employer share of the employees until such time that the excess payments for the past few years be utilized/consumed for the employer share contributions. Coordination with the HDMF personnel is to be done with regards to the foregoing recommendation.

11.6	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly BRSs, financial statements, trial balances and subsidiary ledgers in compliance with the provisions of the foregoing laws and regulations.
12.7 	Direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to fast track the construction and completion of the evacuation facility that can be used in times of calamities and other disasters.
13.6 	We recommended that management direct the concerned employees in the fast implementation of the two (2) BUB-DA projects that can be used by the beneficiaries in their farming endeavors.

14.6 	We recommended that Management direct the concerned employees to fasten the implementation of the potable water system project to comply the mandate of the SALINTUBIG program and as partner of the government in progress and development.

Further, Management may request for write-off for those AOs who were already dead and/or can no longer be traced pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
1.6		We recommended that Management adhere to the guidelines set forth in Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular no. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and that Management exert extra effort for the enforcement of liquidation of the said cash advances to enable the LGU to provide accurate reports to the public;
1.7		It is also recommended that Management withhold the salaries and other emoluments of the Accountable Officers for failure to render liquidation and settlement of the long outstanding cash advances. Further, Management may request for write-off for those AOs who were already dead and/or can no longer be traced pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

4.13	We reiterate our recommendation that Management requires the concerned personnel to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, to confirm the existence and accuracy of reported Inventory accounts balances in the Financial Statements.
4.14	Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the stock cards maintained in the General Services Office.

5.6		We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make necessary adjustments, if any.

6.8		We recommended that Management exert extra effort by finding alternative ways aside from sending demand letters to the said barangays for the immediate liquidation of the long outstanding downloaded funds to determine if the purpose for which it has been transferred has been served and demand return of the fund if found out not utilized or used.
6.9		It is also recommended instruct the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to its proper account on the financial assistance given to Liga ng mga Barangay amounting to P7,500.00.

7.6		We recommended that Management exert extra effort through making follow ups for the liquidation of the outstanding fund transfer and demanding return of the fund if found not utilized or has been used not in accordance with Memorandum of Agreement.

8.8		We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to substantiate the reconciling items presented in BRS and prepare immediately the journal entry voucher/s for its adjustment;
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasury Office to reconcile the ledger and cashbook regularly. Further, the Municipal Treasurer record all valid reconciling items in the cashbook and find alternative ways to substantiate bank reconciling items to prove the errors made by the AGDB.
Review the Memorandum of Agreement with GSIS and stop the practice of paying insurance thru direct IRA deduction; and
Communicate with the authorized depository banks for possible adjustments of bank errors and ask copy for the bank memos for the details of other DM/CM reconciling items.

9.6		We recommended that the management effect the necessary adjustment in the contract cost and/or deduct the total excess amount from the billing of the contractor.
9.7		It is also recommended to meticulously review the prepared Program of Works (POWs) and its unit cost analysis, which is the basis for the Approved Budget for Contract, if the cost estimates conformed with standard engineering practice & agency’s standard capabilities.  Further, see to it that all items reflected in the approved POW were properly implemented in order to avoid disallowance in audit.

10.5	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay of not less than Four Thousand pesos (Php4,000.00) to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for their daily needs until the next payday.

11.7	We recommended that management require the concerned accountable officer to liquidate fully the cash advances made for payroll and refund any unexpended balances to the Collecting Officer/Cashier who will issue the necessary official receipt within five days after 15th day and end of the month pay period.
11.8	It is also recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant review the payroll before allowing withdrawal of cash advances so that only net amount will be drawn in order to safeguard possible loss or misuse of government funds.

13.5	We recommended that Management stop the practice of allowing any employee to handle two or more incompatible functions and ensure that sound internal control system be installed in every operation of the municipality to minimize the chance of undetected risk that may result to loss of government funds.

14.6	We recommended that the Management strengthen its Local Council for the Protection of Children by providing the required fund which is one percent of the IRA of the Municipality to support the implementation of its projects, program and activities and strictly adhere to Section 15 of RA 9344 or the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

16.8	We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The Municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

17.8	We recommended that management:
Stop sending officials and employees to seminars and trainings which do not relate in the performance of their duties;
Stop the practice of allowing employees to reimburse actual claims for travelling expenses without certification by the head of agency as absolutely necessary in the performance of their assignment; and
Regulate and scrutinize the travel claims of every officials and employees.

18.6	We recommended that Management:
Limit the hiring of job orders/contractuals as to what is necessary for the office to perform its mandated functions effectively and efficiently;
Require the Human Resource Management Office to make a thorough study on the manpower needs of every office and to prepare a report thereon addressed to the Municipal Mayor to serve as guide in the determination of the number of job orders/contractuals; and
Conduct regular monitoring on the work accomplishments of hired workers.

19.8	We recommend that management:
To refrain from granting honorarium or incentives without legal basis;
Allocate amount for its RATA under Personal Services account; and
Strictly adhere to provisions of the General Appropriation Act on honoraria.

20.5	We recommend that Management monitor regularly all its loan repayments to avoid excessive appropriation and double payments so that other equally important projects can be funded during the year.

22.9	We recommend that Management exert extra effort in 1) hiring additional technical personnel to augment the manpower of the implementing office 2) finding alternative ways on how to fast track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects and 3) strict monitoring of project implementation to ensure completion of the project by setting time frames and target dates so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the municipality.

24.6	We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of LDRRMF for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation pursuant to DILG DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
24.7	It is also recommended that Management allocate funds for the implementation of infrastructure projects which can minimize the risk of flood during heavy rains and/or can lessen the damage dealt by an occurring calamity.

25.5	We recommended that Management utilize the fund donated for the rehabilitation and post disaster recovery activities for the restoration and/or improvement of the livelihood and living condition of disaster-affected communities, including efforts in reducing disaster risk factors in accordance with the principle “Build back better”.
25.6	In the future, any funds transferred from other agencies intended for provisions of immediate needs of the disaster affected constituents, it is recommended to utilize the fund immediately for its intended purpose.

26.6	We recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund budget in a year in order to attain the objectives for which the fund was intended.

27.6	We recommend that Management maximize the utilization of the GAD fund for the year so that wider scope of intended beneficiaries can be benefited of the GAD programs, projects and activities, and objective of the fund can be fully achieved during the year.
27.7	It is also recommended to fast track the implementation of projects attributable to GAD.

7.4     Instruct the Municipal Budget Office to properly review the AIP and appropriately include in the Annual Budget all Programs, Activities and Projects written therein.

8.9   We recommended that the SB Office be directed to stop the extravagant claim of cell card allowance and the MBO to scrutinize the budget prepared by each department to avoid claims which are considered to be without restraint, judiciousness and economy.

9.6     Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit regularly to COA the Monthly Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations  to ensure timely  review and verification  of the same.

10.5   Instruct the LCPC Secretariat to exert effort in the establishment and maintenance of a complete database for children in order to create an effective/responsive LCPC PAPs for the best interest of the children.

11.5   Direct the Municipal Accounting Office that the DVs and its supporting documents of the above cash advances be submitted to the Office of the ATL and regularly submit, within seven (7) working days from the release of check, the same documents each time a cash advance for confidential fund is granted in conformity with the provision of the above-mentioned joint circular.

We recommend that management:

Advise the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate in informing payees of the availability of checks and the need to surrender the checks, for cancellation, which became stale in the hands of the payee.

We recommend that Management send demand letters to barangays for the immediate liquidation of the fund transferred if the purpose for which it has been transferred has been served.

We recommend that Management exert maximum effort to collect the long outstanding loan receivables due from identified beneficiaries through sending demand letters and statement of account and follow up to enforce collection.

Further, we recommend that the Municipal Account maintain supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the stock cards maintained in the Treasurer’s Office or the General Services Office.

We recommend that management a) instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual count of all PPE items including the preparation and submission of the inventory report/ RPCPPE; b) require the Property Unit to maintain property cards indicating the details, quantities, exact date of acquisition, and status/condition of all PPE items; and c) initiate disposal of the unserviceable properties.

Direct the General Services Officer to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular 92-386, in order to prevent further deterioration and generate additional income from their disposal.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of charging the monetization of leave credits to Lumpsum appropriation for Terminal Leave and strictly adhere to the Joint Circular No. 2-97 set by Civil Service Commission and Department of Budget and Management.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to check religiously the computation of the claims for travelling expenses and per diems to avoid disallowance in audit. Further, direct the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess claim as shown in the Annex

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services on reimbursement basis, except petty purchases to be paid out of the Petty Cash Fund.
We further recommended that Management instruct the Bid and Awards Committee to strictly implement the use of any of the alternative methods of procurement provided under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, if public bidding is not feasible.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to require the concerned payee or the processor of the subject DVs to submit the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation in audit. Henceforth, any payment of claims should be supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and regularity of transactions, otherwise the same shall be subjected to suspension or disallowance in audit.

It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the basic supporting documents necessary to prove the validity, propriety and legality of any disbursement from municipality’s as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management require the responsible officials to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009 by informing COA Auditor through written request or submission of delivery receipts of inventories, specifically consumable items on or within 24 hours after delivery of procured inventories or materials for proper checking as to the correctness/accuracy and existence of deliveries.

We recommend that the management effect the necessary adjustment on the contract cost or the excess contract amount will be deducted from the billing of the contractor. Further, submit to the Office of the Auditor the status of management action taken on the deficiencies noted.
Further, meticulously review the prepared POWs and its unit cost analysis, which is the basis for the Approved Budget for Contract, if the cost estimates conformed with standard engineering practice, proper use of the ACEL rates & agency’s standard capabilities in order to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommend that Management requires the Municipality's BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
We recommend that Management submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report within 5 days from end of January of the preceding year.

We recommend that Management hire additional technical personnel to augment the manpower of the implementing office in order to fast track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects. In addition, the Local Development Committee should consider and prioritize development projects that are readily implementable without unresolved issues to prevent further delays in the implementation and completion of the project.

It is also recommended that the Local Development Committee optimize the utilization of 20 percent Development Fund through the following:

Proper planning of project including timing of execution;

Coordinated action from LGU officials for better result; and

Strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the project by setting time frames and target dates so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the municipality.
We recommend that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the water system projects so that barangay beneficiaries can enjoy the safe and potable water.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineering Office strictly observe the afore-cited regulation to furnish the Office of the Auditor of certified copies of the suspension orders for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.
Further, the MEO should ensure that projects will be completed within the construction period as stipulated in the contract to provide the constituents with a better road accessibility.

We recommend that the management instruct the MSWDO to:

Fully implement and utilize funds downloaded and to be downloaded by the DSWD for Supplemental Feeding Program in order to attain the objectives of the fund; and

Expedite the submission of the liquidation after every activity in order to have continuous implementation of the program; and 

Strictly adhere to existing rules and regulations on disbursements of SFP fund to avoid delay in the implementation of the program.
We recommend that Management instruct the implementing office to fast track the implementation of the projects so that targeted beneficiaries can enjoy the intended benefits may derived therefrom.

We recommend that the management instruct the Municipal Local School Board to appropriate in the succeeding years for the construction/repair/rehabilitation/ improvement of school buildings and other facilities so that a wider scope of the beneficiaries specifically the school children can be benefited.
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical inventory-taking of the above enumerated PPE and submit the required RPCPPE to the COA on or before January 31 of every ensuing year.

We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical inventory-taking of all items included under PPE, as well as those items considered as small tangible items, regularly to establish the existence and proper valuation of recorded assets, and fairly report the balances of the PPE accounts. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging expenditures that are not related to projects on social and economic development, and environmental outcomes, and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures to the 20% development fund. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

Likewise, expenditures such as payment of labor, direct materials and overhead pertaining to the construction, rehabilitation or repair of agency assets/government structures, which could be an addition, could contribute to the betterment, or could extend the life of such assets, should be capitalized. Also, use of appropriate accounts in recording fund transfers and other transactions shall be made to clearly disclose the nature of the transactions actually undertaken by the Municipality to avoid inaccurately reported account balances and misleading information in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management ensure that fuel purchased by the Municipality should only be issued to government vehicles. In no case shall it be fueled to privately owned motor vehicles. Instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to examine all driver’s trip tickets to ensure that the fuel purchased are loaded only to government vehicles. Moreover, the use of government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a driver’s trip ticket, which should be serially numbered and should bear the approval of the Head of Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence. Only originally signed trip tickets shall be submitted to the COA. 

Furthermore, ensure that all payments of fuel expenses are completely documented with these trip tickets, and instruct the drivers to properly and completely fill-up all the necessary data therein such as the no. of liters of fuel purchased, utilized and unused before and after the trip. Data on speedometer readings, names of passengers, and vehicle used, the plate no. of which shall be indicated, shall likewise be disclosed. These shall be summarized at the end of every month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels and a monthly report of fuel consumption, and be submitted to the Office of the Auditor to enable the audit of fuel expenses.

It is also recommended to verify and monitor the implementation of projects funded out of the transferred funds especially for long outstanding downloaded funds and demand return of the fund if found not utilized or used.

In view of the failure of the concerned AOs to adhere to the existing rules and regulations on handling and reporting of collections, the Audit Team recommend the following courses of actions:

a) Direct the Revenue Collectors to remit their collections intact and on time to the LO in order to reflect the correct amount of income and other affected account in the books of the LGU.

b) Require them to explain the reasons for the delay in reporting and remittance, in order to know the cause of continued practice which may be used as basis in the formulation of policies on imposition of sanctions to erring accountable officers.

c) Also, require them to record the collection and remittance in the books on the actual date it was collected and remitted to generate a reliable cash book balance. 

d) The Municipal Treasurer, being the custodian of the accountable forms, should ensure that the requisitioning collector is clear with his/her accountabilities prior to issuance of another accountable forms, to avoid accumulation of unremitted collections.

e) Require the concerned collector to apply for bond should he continue to be a collector, as required in Section 305 (f) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction:
In view of the failure of the concerned AOs to adhere to the existing rules and regulations on handling and reporting of collections, the Audit Team recommend the following courses of actions:

a) Direct the Revenue Collectors to remit their collections intact and on time to the LO in order to reflect the correct amount of income and other affected account in the books of the LGU.

b) Require them to explain the reasons for the delay in reporting and remittance, in order to know the cause of continued practice which may be used as basis in the formulation of policies on imposition of sanctions to erring accountable officers.

c) Also, require them to record the collection and remittance in the books on the actual date it was collected and remitted to generate a reliable cash book balance. 

d) The Municipal Treasurer, being the custodian of the accountable forms, should ensure that the requisitioning collector is clear with his/her accountabilities prior to issuance of another accountable forms, to avoid accumulation of unremitted collections.

e) Require the concerned collector to apply for bond should he continue to be a collector, as required in Section 305 (f) of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit immediately the required BRSs. Strictly comply with the requirement on the submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements as provided in Item 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 and submit the same, together with the bank statements, on or before due dates to the Office of the Auditor. Furthermore, require the reconciliation of the general and subsidiary ledgers pertaining to the subject trust fund accounts. Periodic reconciliation of cash balances shall likewise be conducted by the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate.
observations.managementAction: Periodic reconciliation of CIB-LCCA balances was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management execute a MOA with the Provincial Government of Dinagat Islands and with the aforementioned recipient-barangays. A copy of the MOAs shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Everyfinancial assistance granted thereafter shall be supported with a MOA. For financial assistance granted to barangays, these shall be recorded in the books of accounts as a receivable to compel the recipient-barangays to liquidate the funds transferred within the prescribed period to ensure that the funds are utilized for the intended purpose(s) and the implementation of which are actually undertaken and monitored.
observations.managementAction: Every financial assistance were now supported with MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC Chairman and members to carefully plan the procurement of goods and services to ensure compliance with the proper use of public bidding and the alternative methods of procurement. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to examine the supporting documents and ensure that these are proper, valid and complete before certifying on the disbursement vouchers. Proper withholding of taxes shall likewise be effected. Direct also the Municipal Treasurer to require all suppliers and creditors to issue the necessary official receipts upon issuance of checks to ensure that they are BIR-registered and bona fide to transact with the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Procurement was now carefully planned in conformity with RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the concerned employees to properly accomplish their applications for leave. Moreover, instruct the Human Resource Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to verify the validity, correctness and completeness of the supporting documents, and the immediate supervisors of the concerned employees to sign the recommending approval portion of the said application before approval and payment.
observations.managementAction: Some payments for monetization of leave were still not supported with complete documentations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging expenditures that are not related to projects on social and economic development, and environmental outcomes, and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures to the 20% development fund. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

Likewise, expenditures such as payment of labor, direct materials and overhead pertaining to the construction, rehabilitation or repair of agency assets/government structures, which could be an addition, could contribute to the betterment, or could extend the life of such assets, should be capitalized. Also, use of appropriate accounts in recording fund transfers and other transactions shall be made to clearly disclose the nature of the transactions actually undertaken by the Municipality to avoid inaccurately reported account balances and misleading information in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management still charged minimal expenses not related to the socio-economic and environmental outcomes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories and instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to bring the balances of the said inventory accounts to correct balances. Regular conduct of the semestral physical count shall be made thereafter, and periodic reconciliation of accounting records and records maintained by the Municipal Treasurer or the concerned personnel designated as supply officer shall be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made by the accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MPDC to submit the required list to enable the Auditor to validate the existence and status of implementation of the related projects. Likewise, promptly submit on a quarterly basis the list of publicized programs, activities and projects as required in the above-cited COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: Submitted on a quarterly basis the list of publicized programs, activities and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to carefully review the budget for confidential fund to comply with the provisions of the above-cited Joint Circular. Actual charges against the said fund shall likewise be reviewed before effecting the payment thereof by both the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that only valid confidential expenses are incurred. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to strictly follow the procedural guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances for CF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Perpetual Inventory System shall be followed, and that payment of supplies purchased shall be recorded by debiting the appropriate inventory account. In recording the corresponding supplies expenses accounts, the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of the Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from the Municipal Treasurer shall be used as basis. Moreover, computation of the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory shall be made using the moving average method.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of materials and supplies were now inventoried.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure that fuel purchased by the Municipality should only be issued to government vehicles. In no case shall it be fueled to privately owned motor vehicles. Instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to examine all driver’s trip tickets to ensure that the fuel purchased are loaded only to government vehicles. Moreover, the use of government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a driver’s trip ticket, which should be serially numbered and should bear the approval of the Head of Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence. Only originally signed trip tickets shall be submitted to the COA. 

Furthermore, ensure that all payments of fuel expenses are completely documented with these trip tickets, and instruct the drivers to properly and completely fill-up all the necessary data therein such as the no. of liters of fuel purchased, utilized and unused before and after the trip. Data on speedometer readings, names of passengers, and vehicle used, the plate no. of which shall be indicated, shall likewise be disclosed. These shall be summarized at the end of every month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels and a monthly report of fuel consumption, and be submitted to the Office of the Auditor to enable the audit of fuel expenses.
observations.managementAction: There were still driver’s trip tickets that were not properly accomplished by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the requirement on the submission of financial reports and transaction documents as provided in Section 6.05 of COA Circular 95-006 and submit financial reports and documents on or before due dates to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted best effort to submit all DVs and financial reports on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit immediately the required BRSs. Strictly comply with the requirement on the submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements as provided in Item 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 and submit the same, together with the bank statements, on or before due dates to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Not all BRSs were submitted on time by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MPDC to submit the required list to enable the Auditor to validate the existence and status of implementation of the related projects. Likewise, promptly submit on a quarterly basis the list of publicized programs, activities and projects as required in the above-cited COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted quarterly publicized PAPs but not the status of all on-going projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to record expenditures using the appropriate account to reflect the accurate amount of the affected accounts for the period ended. Moreover, direct the Municipal Budget Officer to exercise due judgment in the account classification, based on the nature and transactions, specified conditions and purpose to ensure that obligations incurred are charged to the appropriate accounts and to facilitate reconciliation of records between the accounting and budget office with regard to obligation accounting. Sound financial plan and budget shall be implemented.
observations.reason: There were still expenditures that were recorded using inappropriate accounts and charged against appropriations from other expense items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the submission of official receipts, invoices, statement of market purchases, and other documents that could substantiate actual purchases of food supplies used in the implementation of the supplemental feeding program. Submission of pictures or any other documentation that could confirm that the feeding activities were actually conducted shall likewise be made. Subsequent distribution of food supplies to target beneficiaries under the SFP shall be completely documented.
observations.managementAction: Official receipts, invoices, statement of market purchases, and other documents that could substantiate actual purchases of food supplies used in the implementation of the supplemental feeding program were now submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the requirement on the submission of financial reports and transaction documents as provided in Section 6.05 of COA Circular 95-006 and submit financial reports and documents on or before due dates to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend to direct Municipal Accountant to follow strictly Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 by withholding the salaries of the AOs until full liquidation of cash advance is made and refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advance/s.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant did not to grant additional cash advances to officials and employees who have existing cash advances that are not liquidated yet.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to follow strictly the provision of Section 18 of the IRR of RA 9184 that specification for the procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the concerned personnel to follow strictly the guidelines in preparing the procurement of goods, supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of paying disbursement without the signatures/approval of the vouchers of the officials concerned pursuant to Section 4 paragraph 5 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers (DVs) before submission to COA were all signed by signatories.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management: 

(a) refrain from disbursing funds not appropriated for food and/or representation expenses but rather provide for representation expenses in the future budgets and, 

(b) instruct the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to properly record the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Food and representation expenses were now charged to the representation expenses appropriated by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend consistent, full, and complete compliance with the requirements of the Full Disclosure Policy required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134, to ensure transparency on its operations and transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management required the Office of the Municipal Accountant to consistently and comply with the requirements of the Full Disclosure Policy.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management include in their GAD Plan and Budget the clearly identified programs, activities and projects that are gender -responsive governance affecting their clients and personnel based on their mandates and functions. Moreover, the Municipal Government, through a policy directive create a committee that will be responsible in the identification, implementation and monitoring of the GAD program and activities conformably with the Joint Circular No. 2001-1.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget  for CY 2016 had identified programs, activities and projects that are gender-responsive governance affecting their clients and personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the immediate transfer of unutilized LDRRMF of CY 2012 to a special trust fund account and continue doing the same for the next years’ unutilized LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: Comply strictly with Section 5 of RA 10121 to keep the public informed on the implementation and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to the transparency and full disclosure policy of the government. Likewise, direct the LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the fund as required in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality posted quarterly reports on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) on the Full Disclosure Policy Board.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of purchasing supplies and materials on reimbursement basis; follow strictly Article XVI of IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement of supplies and other various expenses were still made during the CY 2016 on a minimal manner.

observations.reason: Various non-priority expenditures were still charged to the SEF during the CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: Advise the accountable officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate their unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, withhold their salaries and apply other sanctions pursuant to paragraph 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant not to grant advances to elected officials other than for travelling expenses and strictly comply with Sections 4.1.2 to 4.1.4 of the aforementioned circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed the concerned employees and officials of their unliquidated cash advances and sent demand letters through the heads of their respective departments. There were partial liquidations made on said cash advances.
